extra.reasoning:
The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on every reachable execution.

Key observations:
1. sent becomes true ONLY via t2 (send_attempt), whose guard requires not opted_out. So whenever sent is set true, opted_out is false at that moment.
2. opted_out becomes true ONLY via t1, which simultaneously sets sent:=false. So at the instant opted_out flips to true, sent is false.
3. opted_out is monotonic: no transition ever sets opted_out back to false. Once true, it stays true.
4. Once opted_out is true, t2 (the only sent:=true transition) is permanently disabled (its guard not opted_out is false). Every event in this region has an enabled transition that sets sent:=false: optout->t1 (sent:=false), send_attempt->t3 (sent:=false), tick->t4 (sent:=false). There is no enabled transition in the opted_out=true region that leaves sent at true or sets it true.

Therefore no reachable state has opted_out=true and sent=true simultaneously. The argument is independent of the 9-event bound. The antecedent (opted_out) is only ever true in states where sent is forced false, so the implication always holds.
